checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture (lines 16-39) gives a Mermaid flowchart with named component boundaries in an 'upstream' subgraph (DM, DI, DR, DRL, CMV, HA) and a 'downstream' subgraph of existing components (DS, B, RL, QG, WR), an explicit integration narrative (line 38: 'Flow stays unidirectional...feedback returns only via WFI/diagnose into a new domain-model update run'), and the ## Layer Specifications table (lines 60-67) plus ## Components table (lines 41-56) name technology per layer (SKILL.md, PowerShell+bash, JSON Schema, existing sdd-hook-guard.js/.py). Frontend layer is a reasoned N/A (no graphical frontend), consistent with frontend-spec.md. All three required elements present for this feature type.

Dependency chain in ## Architecture is linear/acyclic: DM -> DI/DR -> DRL -> CMV -> HA -> DS -> B -> RL -> QG -> WR. The only feedback edge (WR -.-> DM, line 35) is explicitly scoped in the text (line 39) as routing into a new, separate 'domain-model update' invocation rather than a synchronous re-entrant dependency, so it does not constitute a cycle in the component dependency graph.

## Data Plan (lines 89-105) contains all three required sub-fields: 'Data Entities:' (bounded contexts, terms, aggregates, relations, events, commands, policies, message flows), 'Existing Data Affected:' ('none (new domain/ directory; additive Bounded-Context: field...)'), and 'Migration Strategy:' ('none required...absence keeps legacy behavior (AC-010)').

## API / Contract Plan (lines 107-117) specifies the new contracts/domain-contract.v1.schema.json envelope shape, states that verdict outputs reuse existing review-contract.v1 and cross-model-verdict/v1 schemas unchanged, and explicitly states 'No HTTP APIs.' This satisfies the 'no API changes' / contract-only requirement for a CLI/skill feature with no HTTP surface.

## Security Boundaries (lines 135-143) is present as a table naming trust boundaries (seed inputs -> interviewer, agent -> approval lines, domain-contract.json -> gates), an auth/authz mechanism column for each row (content-as-data rule, hook guard line-diff rejection, JSON Schema validation), a data-classification column (internal / n/a / internal), and an OWASP-concerns column (Injection (prompt), Broken Access Control, Software/Data Integrity). It also cross-references security-spec.md#trust-boundaries, which independently provides a full STRIDE table, OWASP mapping, and data-classification detail. No required item is missing.

SKIP: design.md declares 'Feature Type: framework plugin (new upstream lane in an existing multi-plugin repository)', which is not literally one of the four enumerated skip values, but design.md contains no ## Frontend Plan or ## Backend Plan section (it uses ## Components/## Architecture instead), and frontend-spec.md is an exhaustive, reasoned N/A ('no graphical frontend, no web runtime, and no client-side state'; Component Tree, State Shape, Routes and Components, API Client Strategy all N/A). There is no frontend surface or frontend-declared endpoint/state-management assumption to cross-check against the API / Contract Plan, so the scoped surface for this check does not exist (calibration Cannot-Verify Handling: SKIP when 'the scoped surface does not exist').

## Test Strategy (lines 119-133) covers unit scope (Pester: schema validation, conformance-check, guard line-diff, template-language, validate-repository fixtures), integration scope (end-to-end artifact-set generation, domain-sync injection, absence regression, update-mode re-run, cross-model gate fixtures), and acceptance-criterion verification is traceable via acceptance-tests.md's Test ID column (TEST-001..TEST-017) mapping each AC to a named test file/fixture (e.g. tests/sdd-domain/artifact-set.Tests.ps1). Concrete artifact names are given throughout (e.g. tests/hooks/domain-approval-guard.Tests.ps1, reports/domain-review/ fixture run). No fullstack graphical user journey exists (ux-spec.md/frontend-spec.md confirm CLI-only), so no separate E2E/user-journey artifact is required beyond the named integration fixtures, which already cover the end-to-end pipeline (seven-stage generation through cross-model gate).

All components referenced in ## Architecture (DM, DI, DR, DRL, CMV, HA, DS, B, RL, QG, WR) are either defined in the ## Components table (domain-model, domain-interviewer, domain-reverse, domain-review-loop, domain-sync, domain-reviewer-a/b, check-domain-conformance, contracts/domain-contract.v1.schema.json, Hook guard extension, cross-model-verify) or are named, established existing sdd-forge components described inline in the diagram/narrative and corroborated by requirements.md (bootstrap Phase 1, review loops, quality-gate, workflow-retrospective — all referenced in requirements.md Main Workflows and Goals sections). No undefined component reference found.

design.md ## ADR Change Log (lines 83-87) lists ADR-0004 ('DDD upstream lane as seventh plugin with seven-stage pipeline, cross-model-verified domain gate, warn-first conformance', Status: Accepted). The referenced file docs/adr/0004-ddd-upstream-domain-lane.md exists on disk (verified via directory listing). The feature's core architectural departure (new plugin lane, cross-model-verified gate, warn-first conformance escalation) is fully covered by this single ADR; no additional undocumented architectural decision was found in design.md.

design.md ## Design System Compliance (line 71) states 'N/A — ds_profile: none'. This is corroborated by ux-spec.md ## Design Tokens ('N/A — ds_profile: none (repository has no design-system/)') and by direct verification that no design-system/ directory exists in the repository root. The feature ships no graphical UI (frontend-spec.md: 'no graphical frontend, no web runtime, and no client-side state'), so there is no design-token or component-library surface for a design system to govern. The N/A declaration is reasoned and consistent across all three artifacts that reference it; no conformance gap found.
